description_long: This is a vintage-style oil lantern with a weathered, rusted metal frame and a clear glass enclosure at its center. The lantern features a cylindrical glass chamber protected by a wire guard, with a prominent round base and a domed top vent. Two sturdy vertical arms connect the base to the top, providing structural support and a handle for carrying or hanging. The surface of the metal is heavily textured, showing signs of age and oxidation, giving it an antique, utilitarian appearance. A small knob and lever mechanism are visible on the front, likely for adjusting the wick or flame. The overall design is functional, robust, and evocative of classic lanterns used in outdoor or industrial urban settings.
description: A weathered, antique oil lantern with a rusted metal frame and clear cylindrical glass enclosure, featuring a wire guard and functional adjustment knob.
description_view_0: Front view showing the adjustment knob, wire guard, and clear glass chamber prominently.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical support arm and the curvature of the glass enclosure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical metal arms and the back of the glass chamber.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the support arm and wire guard visible, similar to view 1 but mirrored.
